strategy: Break of Structure + Swing High/Low + POI

market_structure.swing_high: A candle high that is higher than N candles on both sides — marks potential resistance and BOS level
market_structure.swing_low: A candle low that is lower than N candles on both sides — marks potential support and BOS level
market_structure.bos_bullish: Price closes ABOVE a confirmed swing high → structure shifts bullish, trend is up
market_structure.bos_bearish: Price closes BELOW a confirmed swing low → structure shifts bearish, trend is down
market_structure.choch: Change of Character — first BOS against the prevailing trend, signals potential reversal

poi_types.order_block_bullish: Last bearish candle (down close) before a bullish BOS — price likely returns to this zone to rebalance
poi_types.order_block_bearish: Last bullish candle (up close) before a bearish BOS — price likely returns to this zone to rebalance
poi_types.fvg_bullish: Fair Value Gap up: gap between candle[2] high and candle[0] low on an impulsive up move — unmitigated imbalance
poi_types.fvg_bearish: Fair Value Gap down: gap between candle[2] low and candle[0] high on an impulsive down move — unmitigated imbalance
poi_types.premium_discount: Discount zone = below 50% of the swing range (look for longs). Premium zone = above 50% (look for shorts).

bullish: Most recent BOS was bullish (closed above swing high)
Price is in a discount zone (below 50% of swing range)
Price is pulling back into a bullish POI (order block or FVG)
Higher highs and higher lows forming on current timeframe
bearish: Most recent BOS was bearish (closed below swing low)
Price is in a premium zone (above 50% of swing range)
Price is pulling back into a bearish POI (order block or FVG)
Lower highs and lower lows forming on current timeframe
neutral: No confirmed BOS — range-bound between last swing high and swing low
Price is at the 50% equilibrium level
Conflicting structure on higher vs lower timeframe

entry_rules: Only trade in the direction of the most recent BOS
Wait for price to return to a POI (order block or FVG) after BOS
Confirm entry with a lower-timeframe BOS in the direction of the trade at the POI
Bullish entries: POI must be in discount zone (below 50% of range)
Bearish entries: POI must be in premium zone (above 50% of range)
Do not enter if POI has already been mitigated (price traded through it)
risk_rules: Stop loss: below the order block low (bullish trade) or above the order block high (bearish trade)
R:R minimum 1:2 — target next swing high/low or liquidity pool
If 2 losing trades in a row, stop for the day
Do not trade into a POI on the opposite side — wait for mitigation
claude_reads_from_chart: data_get_pine_labels → BOS labels (Bullish BOS / Bearish BOS / CHoCH) with price levels
data_get_pine_lines  → swing high and swing low lines across the chart
data_get_pine_boxes  → POI boxes: bullish/bearish order blocks and FVGs (mitigated vs active)
data_get_pine_tables → current structure bias, last BOS direction, active POIs, discount/premium zone
notes: SMC strategy: BOS confirms trend direction, POIs are the entry zones. Claude reads all drawn levels from the chart via MCP tools.
